lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Yes, JalYt-[caste]-Xo-[player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Inferno.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: TzHaar are born from eggs incubated in lava pools. When hatched, we retain memories. Memories and knowledge passed on from ancestors who returned to the lava.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: When TzHaar hatch, some memories are lost. Those about earliest ancestors are not retained. We experiment.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: This here was once our largest birthing pool. We use it for experiment. We increased the depth at which eggs are incubated. Over time, newly hatched TzHaar had more memories of lost history!

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: But we kept pushing. We wanted answers, but we went too deep.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Eventually the pool collapsed into a sinkhole. At the bottom we found some sort of ancient incubation chamber, from before earliest memories. We now call it the Inferno.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We decide to experiment more. We take eggs from creatures in Fight Cave and incubate them down there.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: When eggs hatched, we see that it was not good decision. They were bigger and stronger, and fought each other for dominance.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Instead of knowledge, we now have chamber of dangerous creatures, and they're not alone. Another being lives in the Inferno, one more powerful than any TzHaar.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We do not know where he came from. All we know is that TzKal-Zuk is hungry. Hungry for memories, memories beyond the Inferno.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We think his goal is the city itself. Lots of TzHaar memories here for him. Luckily, he is trapped in prison down there, and we are keeping him at bay by offering sacrifices.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We fear he can't be defeated, but maybe we can hold back his creatures and keep city safe.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: You, a mere JalYt, showed us he can be stopped though. Thanks to you, we still have hope.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Instead of knowledge, we now have chamber of dangerous creatures. It gets worse, but I don't know if I can trust JalYt-[caste]-Xo-[player name] with this knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I like to think I'm pretty trustworthy. If you tell me, maybe I can help in some way.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: I have one idea. If you sacrifice your fire cape to me, I can take your word.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd like to sacrifice my fire cape.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Very well, just hand it over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I want to keep it for now.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Thank you, JalYt-[caste]-Xo-[player name]. You can enter Inferno now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do down there?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: The creatures down there are dangerous. If you can kill some of them, it would be very helpful to TzHaar.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: But now you can be trusted, I must also give warning. Another being lives in the Inferno, one more powerful than any TzHaar.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We do not know where he came from. All we know is that TzKal-Zuk is hungry. Hungry for memories, memories beyond the Inferno.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We think his goal is the city itself. Lots of TzHaar memories here for him. Luckily, he is trapped in prison down there, and we are keeping him at bay by offering sacrifices.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We fear he can't be defeated, but maybe we can hold back his creatures and keep city safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I definitely think I can help with that.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Thank you, JalYt-[caste]-Xo-[player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll need to take it off first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have a fire cape on me unfortunately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help here in some way?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: I think maybe you can, but first you need to prove I can trust you.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: If you sacrifice your fire cape to me, I can take your word.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Tzhaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: No! TzKal-Zuk's prison is breaking down. There's nothing I can do for you now, JalYt!

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: You are very impressive for a JalYt. You managed to defeat TzKal-Zuk, for now...

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Please accept this cape as a token of appreciation.

sections.title: Upon using a TzKal slayer helmet on him

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Impressive JalYt, but why do you show me this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have conquered the most difficult challenges across Gielinor. But I wish to challenge myself further.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: You wish for more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish to challenge the Inferno on my own terms, without the endorsement of a slayer master.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Hmm. You ask for much, JalYt. But if anybody has proven themselves worthy it is you.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Very well. I shall be the one to endorse your attempts to reach even greater heights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you TzHaar-Ket-Keh. You won't regret it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow what has happened here? That doesn't look good!

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We pushed it too far! JalYt would not understand. The memories, we needed them. Now big Inferno.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: This is our birthing pool. TzHaar are born from eggs incubated in this large pool here.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: When hatched, we retain memories. Memories and knowledge passed on from ancestors who returned to the lava.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something doesn't seem right... There's a large opening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened here?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We went too far... way too far, we need the memories. JalYt would not understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please help me to understand then.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We TzHaar have special ability. When incubated in lava, a hatched TzHaar retains knowledge and memories of ancestors who returned to the lava.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Not all memories though, those about earliest ancestors were not being retained. We experiment.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We increased the depth at which the eggs are incubated. Over time this worked, newly hatched TzHaar had more memories of lost history!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds fantastic though, uncovering the past to determine where your species originated?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Yes, but you JalYt do not understand, we kept pushing it. We wanted answers, but too deep we went.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Eventually the pool collapsed into a sink hole, huge Inferno there. This was big ancient incubation chamber.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We decide to experiment. We take eggs from creatures in Fight Cave and incubate them.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We hatched eggs in there and it was not a good decision. They were so different, bigger, stronger and fought each other for dominance.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Instead of knowledge, we now have a prison of dangerous TzHaar creatures - it gets worse but I cannot trust JalYt with this knowledge yet.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Instead of knowledge, we now have a prison of dangerous creatures... and they're not under our control.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: One grew incredibly strong, powerful. He controls those creatures in the prison. TzKal-Zuk, as we call him...is hungry.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Hungry for memories, memories beyond the Inferno, which only TzHaar in the city can provide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what he's planning? What are you doing?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We can only guess one day he will want to scale up into the city himself. We are keeping him at bay by offering sacrifices for him to feast on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there no way to take him out?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We fear not. But we hoped you, with your fancy JalYt weaponry, would be able to help hold back his creatures. You proved your commitment by sacrificing your fire cape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go down there?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: JalYt, we need help, but this is extremely dangerous. TzHaar not strong enough to take control back.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: We are worried about trusting JalYt to take on this task for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me prove it.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: I have one idea, if you could sacrifice your fire cape to me, I can take your word.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd like to sacrifice my fire cape.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Very well, just hand it over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I want to keep it for now.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Give it your best shot JalYt-[caste]-Xo-[player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me just take it off first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have a fire cape on me unfortunately.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: You can head into The Inferno anytime. We trust you to help us JalYt-[caste]-Xo-[player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a spare infernal cape, perhaps I could offer it back to you for a littlun to take with me?

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Maybe. Hand it over and if you make lucky TokKul toss, it can be yours JalYt.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: But do know, you do not get cape back.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: Luck be a TzHaar tonight. Jal-Nib-Rek is yours.

lines.speaker: TzHaar-Ket-Keh
lines.text: No Jal-Nib-Rek for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nevermind.
